goal: Unify release-tag convention between vendor repo (v*) and monorepo (desktop-v*). Today CLI + framework release on vendor repo v* tags triggering .github/workflows/release-binaries.yml; desktop releases on monorepo desktop-v* tags triggering .github/workflows/desktop-build.yml. Neither auto-triggers the other. Operator must push both tags for a 'complete' release spanning both surfaces. Pick one: (a) cross-repo dispatch — vendor's v* tag fires a repository_dispatch to monorepo which kicks off desktop-build — single push suffices, (b) documented dual-push convention — each release needs both tags, operator explicitly cuts both, naming consistent ('v1.36.0' in vendor = 'desktop-v1.36.0' in monorepo = same release), (c) version pinning — desktop embeds a gad-cli-version in tauri.conf.json, updating it requires a new desktop-v*. Action: (1) operator decide among a/b/c, (2) implement chosen path (cross-repo dispatch if (a), doc-only if (b), version-pin logic if (c)), (3) document the convention in root CLAUDE.md + vendor CLAUDE.md, (4) update release scripts to match. Blocks: clean release story for end users who want 'latest GAD.'
